name: Ensure buckets don't have permissive access policies
body:
### Security intent

This control protects stored data by ensuring that **buckets don't have permissive access policies**.

### Resource state reviewed

The assessment reviews buckets with permissive access policy across AWS and Google Cloud. It focuses on access policy, data protection, retention, or public exposure signals depending on the resource type.

### Data protection expectation

Storage and messaging resources often hold customer data, logs, backups, events, and integration payloads. Access should be explicit, encryption and versioning should be enabled where required, and public or overly broad access should be removed unless there is a documented exception.
remediationDescription:
Depending on the provider and the service you use, check out the following resources:

#### AWS
* [Policies and Permissions in Amazon S3](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-policy-language-overview.html)

#### Google Cloud
* [IAM roles for Cloud Storage](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-permissions)
